title: [Conkeldurr] Battle Guide

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Conkeldurr] is one of the few pure [Fighting] attackers in Champions that trades Speed for raw attrition. 140 Atk and 105 HP off a [sing]le type looks unbalanced, and [iron-fist] turns [drain-punch] into a sustain engine that rebuilds HP while breaking walls. The role is simple — come in late game, stack [bulk-up] once, and [recycle].

## Strengths
[iron-fist] gives [drain-punch], [mach-punch], [ice-punch], and [thunder-punch] a 1.2x damage multiplier, and the [drain-punch] healing makes [Conkeldurr]'s effective HP pool nearly 50 percent larger than the stat bar shows. 140 Atk through [close-combat] hits like a Choice-locked mon without actually locking, and a [sing]le [bulk-up] boost is often enough to clean half a team in the late game. [mach-punch] is the real bandaid on the 45 Speed stat — most [revenge] killers expect to outrun [Conkeldurr] but eat priority first.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Flying], [psychic], and [Fairy] all hit for 2x and [Conkeldurr] loses the Speed race to every one of them. A [Fairy] wall like Clefable or a bulky [Psychic] pivot shuts [drain-punch] off almost entirely, so [knock-off] must be c[lick]ed early to remove their [leftovers]. Burn is the worst problem — a [sing]le [will-o-wisp] [cut]s [iron-fist] Atk in half and the whole sustain plan collapses, which is why [lum-berry] is the best item on opening turns. Against speed control teams ([tailwind], choice-scarf users), [Conkeldurr] can never c[lick] freely.

## Recommended Moveset
[drain-punch] is the locked STAB and the entire identity of the set — [iron-fist], 75 base power, and 50 percent [recover]y rolled into one c[lick]. [mach-punch] covers the Speed deficit and finishes weakened [Electric] or [Rock] threats that would otherwise pick [Conkeldurr] off. [knock-off] is the obligatory cove[rage] slot — [psychic], [Ghost], and pivot walls all lose their items on the way out. [bulk-up] is the fourth slot because one boost turns [Conkeldurr] into a genuine sweeper instead of a pure trade unit. If the team needs more up-front damage, [close-combat] swaps in for [drain-punch], but the sustain loop is gone.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default — it stacks with [drain-punch] and makes [Conkeldurr] nearly impossible to wear down. [focus-sash] is the better pick into matchups full of fast [revenge] killers because it guarantees a [mach-punch] retaliation before going down. [lum-berry] answers Prankster [will-o-wisp] or sleep [powder] users trying to neuter the opening turn. The ability must be [iron-fist] — [guts] needs a self-damage enabler and [sheer-force] [disable]s the [drain-punch] heal. Tera [Steel] is the best primary, covering the [Flying], [psychic], and [Fairy] weaknesses in one go; Tera [Fire] flips the [Flying] and [psychic] matchups into resistances and adds burn immunity, which is a huge gain.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Conkeldurr] needs a reliable pivot partner since it cannot switch in safely against most attackers. An [Electric] [tank] to [absorb] [Flying] hits, a [Dark]-type to eat [psychic] pressure, and a [Poison] or [Steel] breaker to threaten the [Fairy] walls that want to wall [drain-punch]. A dedicated [tailwind] setter fixes the 45 Speed issue outright and turns [Conkeldurr] into a mid-game cleaner. Finally, an [intimidate] ally helps it [soak] physical chip while stacking [bulk-up].

## Play Patterns
Hold [Conkeldurr] for the mid-to-late game — entering turn one hands the opponent a free pivot. Wait until the faster threats are removed or damaged, then bring it in on a forced switch, stack one [bulk-up], and start the [drain-punch] plus [leftovers] cycle. Save [mach-punch] for finishing a low-HP [revenge] killer, not as an opener. Tera only when a [Flying] or [psychic] hit is telegraphed — otherwise keep the option open for the back half of the game.
